title: N.H. police chief placed on leave after video released of him grabbing someone by the throat inside police station
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Gorham Police Chief Jimmy Willhoite is on leave after video shows him grabbing a man by the throat.
Stephen Gregory, who obtained the video, was involved in the altercation with Chief Willhoite inside the police station.
New Hampshire's Department of Justice and the Police Training Council are investigating the incident, officials stated.

title: Man, 29, killed in daytime shooting in Dorchester
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 29-year-old man was shot and killed on Wednesday morning in Dorchester, Boston police confirmed.
Police responded to a gunshot wound incident at 15 York St., where the victim was pronounced dead.
No arrests have been made; authorities urge anyone with information to contact Boston Police CrimeStoppers.

title: U.S. Marshals arrest 2 alleged fugitives weeks after mass shooting in Blue Hill Avenue
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cerron Wilcox-Buford and Laquan Burris were arrested in connection with a June shooting in Mattapan that killed one person.
Wilcox-Buford faces charges of armed assault to murder, while Burris is charged with assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
Both entered not guilty pleas and are held in Suffolk County Jail after failing to post bail, with court hearings set for Aug. 4.

title: Mass. is seeing ‘normal’ levels in cases of the explosive diarrhea parasite despite Midwestern outbreak, DPH says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts has reported 18 cases of cyclospora infection from May 1 to July 1, aligning with normal seasonal numbers.
Midwestern states report over 400 cyclospora cases, but Massachusetts has not seen a similar outbreak, health officials confirm.
State health officials expect more diagnosed cases and will update data in August; cyclospora spreads through contaminated food or water.

title: ‘Being at sea does change a person’: Tall Ships inspire Boston’s young sailors
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 250 features a Parade of Sail, with hundreds of recreational and commercial vessels following a designated route.
Courageous Sailing Center adapted its program by moving upriver to avoid crowds and still provide sailing experiences.
Piers Park Sailing Center and Community Boating Inc. offered youth programs and tours, highlighting sailing's accessibility and educational value.

title: Massachusetts issues statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: MassDEP issued a statewide air quality alert effective until 12 a.m. Thursday due to Canadian wildfire smoke.
Air quality levels in Massachusetts are unhealthy for sensitive groups, including those with heart or lung diseases.
Residents are advised to limit outdoor activities and check MassAir for the latest air quality forecast.

title: Look inside Oosterschelde, the historic Dutch tall ship at Sail Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Oosterschelde, a restored Dutch tall ship, is docked at Rowes Wharf for public tours during Sail Boston 2026.
Visitors can explore the ship's deck, bedrooms, kitchen, and salon during daily touring hours from 10 a.m. to 10 p.m.
After Sail Boston, Oosterschelde will continue giving tours in Quincy before heading to Canada, Spain, and France.

title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room after an Outlaw Music Fest performance in 2024.
Dylan was impressed by Spencer's music and eventually invited her to join his summer tour in 2026.
Spencer, a vocalist blending R&B and country, opens for Dylan in Boston on July 16 and Gilford on July 18.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Five New England hotel restaurants made OpenTable's 2026 list of top 100 in America.
Massachusetts' Contessa and Mooo Beacon Hill, and Maine's Earth at Hidden Pond were highlighted.
Michael Jordan’s Steak House in Connecticut was noted for its upscale steak and seafood offerings.

title: Revolution’s Marko Mitrović reflected on World Cup memories
author: Sam Lattof
bullets: Marko Mitrović recalls watching the World Cup as a child growing up in Belgrade, starting with the 1982 tournament.
Mitrović shared a memory from the 1990 World Cup when attention shifted from his aunt's wedding to a Yugoslavia-Argentina match.
Mitrović, now coaching New England Revolution, was assistant head coach when Serbia won the 2015 FIFA U20 World Cup.
